message:
refactor(installer): remove auto-hook installation, add legacy cleanup

Major installer refactor removing the pre-tool hook auto-configuration
that was introduced in earlier versions. Hooks for Claude Code (PreToolUse)
and Gemini CLI (BeforeTool) are no longer auto-injected into settings.json
during installation.

Instead, the installer now includes idempotent cleanup logic that removes
installer-managed legacy hook entries from prior installations, scoped
strictly to entries the installer itself created (path-scoped,
non-destructive).

Key changes:
- install.sh: Remove ~100 lines of hook detection/injection code including
  json_escape_string(), hook setup functions, and settings.json merging.
  Add cleanup_legacy_settings_entries() for safe removal of old hook refs.
- uninstall.sh: Remove hook-removal code, simplify to binary/alias/skill
  cleanup with TypeScript pi restoration.
- tests/installer_regression.sh: Update test expectations to match new
  installer behavior (no hook assertions, updated cleanup verification).
- README.md: Remove documentation of auto-hook configuration; note that
  legacy entries are cleaned up during upgrades.
- AGENTS.md: Update RCH section to remove PreToolUse hook references;
  clarify that rch should be used via explicit manual offload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
